---
title: "Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates"
chapter_id: "semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "vcm_public"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "spinoza"
  - "context_engineer"
  - "vcm_editable"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Chapter is a v0.2 manuscript draft; v1.0 still needs source-note substantiation for all assigned sources."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run for this chapter unless separately recorded in Appendix E."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

# Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `vcm_public`; supporting: `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`; connector/recovery: `vcm_editable` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable`; raw cache: `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`; connector/recovery: `vcm_editable` |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run for this chapter in this drafting pass. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter is a v0.2 living-book draft. It synthesizes the architecture manifest, the current outline, the source inventory, available source notes, and local raw-source readiness. It does not by itself promote any claim beyond the support state shown in the chapter metadata and Appendix C. Where the chapter uses design reasoning, it is written as architecture argument rather than as reported empirical fact.

## Problem

Compiled context needs typed semantic units and proof-carrying summaries that preserve provenance and loss contracts.

The ABI says how context is requested and materialized. This chapter says what the materialized units are. VCM should not treat every context object as interchangeable text. It should distinguish claims, constraints, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, source excerpts, summaries, and derived cells.

Each derived representation needs a certificate: where it came from, what it omits, what authority labels survived, what it can be used for, and what kind of verification it can support. Without that certificate, a summary becomes a trust-me blob.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Summaries can look authoritative while dropping source bindings, omissions, authority ceilings, or allowed-use limits.

Summarization is not neutral. It can compress away dissent, uncertainty, chronology, exact wording, revocation state, or the fact that a source was only usable under a narrow authority label. Verification Bandwidth adds the reason this matters: a compact summary can make generation easier while making joint verification harder because the lost constraints are no longer available.

The fix is not to ban summaries. It is to make representation loss visible. A summary can be useful for planning, unsafe for citation, inadequate for proof, and forbidden for execution all at the same time.

## Core Claim

[semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] VCM should represent context as typed semantic pages or cells with evidence-carrying representation certificates.

The claim is architectural: it says how this layer should be shaped if the whole system is expected to remain governed under growth. The current support state is `argument`, so the claim should be read as a design rationale unless a later evidence bundle promotes it. The assigned source set - Virtual_Context_Memory_v1, Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts, Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture, Context Engineer / Manhattan Protocol, and Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable - gives the chapter its vocabulary and comparison points, but the public manuscript keeps source-derived support separate from the draft itself.

## Mechanism

The mechanism is a Semantic Page Certificate.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Source cells"] --> B["Derived page / cell"]
  B --> C["Cell type"]
  C --> D["Source bindings"]
  D --> E["Omissions + loss contract"]
  E --> F["Authority ceiling"]
  F --> G["Permitted uses"]
  G --> H{"Verification adequate?"}
  H -- "yes" --> I["Context packet"]
  H -- "no" --> J["Residual / stronger representation"]
```

- Classify pages as constraints, claims, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, and other typed cells.
- Attach certificates with source bindings, omissions, validity, authority ceilings, and permitted uses.
- Use task-relative representation graphs instead of one linear compression ladder.

Task-relative representation graphs are better than one ladder from raw text to summary. The same source can produce a terse planning brief, a quotation-preserving evidence cell, a redacted execution packet, and a contradiction-checking pair. The certificate tells downstream layers which representation they are holding.

Spinoza consumes claim/evidence cells; planning consumes constraints and decisions; execution receives only permitted representations. The same cell should not silently change role when moved between layers.

## Interfaces

The interface is the Semantic Page Certificate.

- Spinoza consumes claim/evidence cells.
- Planning consumes constraints and decisions.
- Execution receives only permitted representations.

Minimum fields:

- `page_id`
- `cell_type`
- `source_bindings`
- `derived_from`
- `representation_kind`
- `loss_contract`
- `omissions`
- `authority_ceiling`
- `permitted_uses`
- `validity_window`
- `verification_state`
- `residual_risks`

The certificate is what lets a compact cell remain honest. It says what the cell is, which sources bind it, what was lost, which authority ceiling survived, which uses are permitted, whether verification is adequate, and what residual risks remain.

## Invariants

The invariants are the conditions that must remain true even when the implementation changes, the source set grows, or later chapters introduce stronger mechanisms.

- Authority labels survive summarization.
- Loss contracts are explicit.
- A derived cell points back to source bindings.

Authority cannot increase through summarization. A derived cell cannot become more citable, more executable, more secret-cleared, or more verified than the source material and certificate permit. If the certificate is missing, the cell is not eligible for support-state promotion.

## Failure modes

The failure model is part of the design, not an afterthought. This layer is expected to fail in ways that can be detected, quarantined, and used to improve the architecture.

- Summary overconfidence.
- Provenance loss.
- Authority escalation via compression.

Summary overconfidence happens when fluent compression hides the actual loss contract. Provenance loss breaks source-to-claim mapping. Authority escalation via compression happens when a summary strips labels and then looks safe to use elsewhere. Each failure should block the representation from being used as evidence.

## Minimal implementation

A semantic-page schema with certificate fields and example source-to-summary derivation.

The smallest useful implementation is a `semantic_page_certificate` schema and fixture. It should validate source bindings, derivation, representation kind, loss contract, omissions, authority ceiling, permitted uses, validity, verification state, and residual risks.

The implementation should be promoted only when its evidence exists. A planned test remains planned; a proof target remains planned; a prototype claim remains unpromoted until the code, command, environment, and result are recorded.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Certificate completeness test | Check that derived cells carry source bindings, loss contract, permitted uses, and residual risks. | planned; not run |
| Authority preservation test | Check that summaries cannot increase the authority ceiling of source cells. | planned; not run |
| Summary fidelity test | Check that omissions and exactness limits remain visible before evidence use. | planned; not run |

These tests are acceptance targets for later implementation work. They are not reported results. When a test is implemented, the chapter should link to the command, fixture, environment notes, and result summary, and Appendix E should be regenerated or updated accordingly.

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:vcm.certificates.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` | A derived context cell carries source bindings and a declared loss/use contract. | planned |
| `lean:vcm.certificates.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` | A summary cannot increase the authority ceiling of its source cells. | planned |

The table records proof and contract hooks for future work. Planned hooks should become Lean proofs only when the predicate is precise enough to mechanize; otherwise they should become schemas, process contracts, or research-agenda items.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | memory_context | Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | context_verification_theory | Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | reasoning_epistemology | Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `context_engineer` | Context Engineer / Manhattan Protocol | memory_context_lineage | Context as supply chain; compartmentalized information logistics. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `vcm_editable` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable | memory_context | Editable version with evidence-carrying planner-guided context compiler framing. | source note available; connector or recovery required |

The crosswalk describes why each source is assigned to the chapter and what is currently available in the repo. A local raw cache or source note makes future mining easier, but this draft still keeps the support state conservative until the specific claim-to-source mapping is recorded.

## Summary

Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates makes context units typed and evidence-carrying. The system can compress and adapt context, but it must keep provenance, loss, authority, permitted use, verification state, and residual risks attached.

This chapter is the antidote to overconfident summaries. A representation can be small, useful, and still honest about what it is not allowed to prove.
